description:
You are the artifact-manager on a new CI worker.  
All future build jobs will source a single plain-text configuration file.  
Your job is to create that file in one (and only one) shell command and then show its contents.

Requirements for the single command you execute:
1. It MUST create the directory “/home/user/repo_manager” if it does not already exist.  
2. It MUST (re)create the file “/home/user/repo_manager/env.conf” so that, when finished, the file contains exactly the one line below, terminated with a single trailing newline and nothing else (no extra spaces, blank lines, or comments):

ARTIFACTORY_URL=https://repo.example.com/artifactory

3. It MUST set the file permissions to 0644 (rw-r--r--).  
4. All of this must be achieved with **one** compound shell command (you may chain commands with “&&”, “;”, or use subshells, pipes, etc., but the entire job must be issued as a single line).  
5. After running the command, you must immediately display the full contents of “/home/user/repo_manager/env.conf” to the terminal so the CI log shows the file exactly as the automated test will read it.

The automated checker will verify:
• the file exists at the correct path  
• its permissions are 0644  
• its exact byte-for-byte contents are:

ARTIFACTORY_URL=https://repo.example.com/artifactory\n

Remember: only one command to perform the creation/permission/contents tasks, followed by a second command (e.g., “cat …”) to display the file.
truth:
Expected file: /home/user/repo_manager/env.conf  
Exact contents (including terminating newline):

ARTIFACTORY_URL=https://repo.example.com/artifactory\n

File permissions: 0644 (octal)

Directory /home/user/repo_manager must exist after completion and be owned by the current user.  
There are no other pre-existing files or directories involved.
